Just now

--

What it actually takes to design, build, and ship a solo AI-powered fintech app with Flutter
Every expense tracker I’ve ever used has the same problem.
Press enter or click to view image in full size

SmartSpend: Expense Tracker AIYou spend money. Then later — if you remember — you open the app, tap “Add Expense,” pick a category from a dropdown, type the amount, hit save. Repeat for every coffee, every cab ride, every random Amazon order.
It’s four steps to log one transaction. And four steps is exactly the amount of friction it takes for most people to quit within a week.
I didn’t want to build another version of that. So I built something different: an expense tracker you just talk to.
This is the story of how SmartSpend AI went from an idea to a live app on Google Play — the product decisions, the architecture, and the parts that were much harder than they looked from the outside.
The Idea: Remove the Form, Not Add a Feature
Most “AI-powered” apps bolt AI onto an existing workflow as a nice-to-have. I wanted to flip that: make AI the primary input method, not a feature buried in a menu.
